items.title: Mini Shai Hulud: Compromised @antv npm packages enable CI/CD credential theft
items.description: Microsoft identified an active supply chain attack targeting the @antv npm package ecosystem. A threat actor compromised an @antv maintainer account and published malicious versions of widely used data-visualization packages, affecting libraries like echarts-for-react with over 1 million weekly downloads. The attack propagates through dependency chains into CI/CD pipelines and cloud workloads. A 4
items.ai_summary: The Mini Shai Hulud supply chain attack compromises the @antv npm package ecosystem by exploiting a maintainer account, allowing the threat actor to publish malicious versions of popular data-visualization packages such as echarts-for-react. This attack affects over 1 million weekly downloads and propagates through dependency chains into CI/CD pipelines and cloud workloads, enabling credential theft. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove and replace compromised @antv packages with known good versions, and implement robust dependency management and monitoring to detect similar attacks. Additionally, CI/CD pipeline credentials should be rotated and access restricted to prevent further exploitation. The attack is attributed to a sophisticated threat actor utilizing T1195 supply chain compromise tactics, highlighting the need for enhanced security measures in the software development supply chain.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Misconfigured, Enrolled and Dormant: Anatomy of a P2Pinfect Kubernetes Compromise
items.description: An investigation identified persistent P2Pinfect botnet presence within Google Kubernetes Engine clusters at multiple organizations, with one compromise lasting six months. The intrusions originated from exposed Redis instances that provided initial access. The botnet utilizes a peer-to-peer architecture for resilience against takedowns and operates as a botnet-for-hire platform. While no second-s
items.ai_summary: The P2Pinfect botnet has been found to compromise Google Kubernetes Engine clusters through exposed Redis instances, providing initial access. This botnet utilizes a peer-to-peer architecture, making it resilient against takedowns, and operates as a botnet-for-hire platform. The compromise can last for extended periods, with one instance lasting six months, allowing for prolonged unauthorized access and potential data breaches. To remediate, defenders should ensure that all Redis instances are properly configured, secured, and monitored, and implement measures such as network segmentation and access controls to limit the spread of the botnet. Additionally, defenders should regularly scan for and address any misconfigured or dormant resources within their Kubernetes clusters.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583.005| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Politicians to Ditch Signal for Homegrown Apps
items.description: European governments are transitioning from encrypted messaging applications like Signal and WhatsApp to sovereign Matrix-based solutions. This shift follows successful phishing campaigns, primarily attributed to Russian intelligence services, exploiting Signal's linked devices feature to gain persistent access to political communications. While Signal was initially recommended for external commun
items.ai_summary: The threat involves phishing campaigns, primarily attributed to Russian intelligence services, exploiting Signal's linked devices feature to gain persistent access to political communications. This vulnerability affects Signal users, particularly those with linked devices, and can lead to the compromise of sensitive information. The real-world impact includes the potential for data breaches and eavesdropping on high-stakes communications, such as those between politicians. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement two-factor authentication, regularly review and update linked devices, and consider transitioning to alternative secure communication platforms like Matrix-based solutions. Additionally, users should be cautious when receiving messages with links or attachments from unknown sources and avoid using public computers or public Wi-Fi to access sensitive information.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: SEO poisoning campaign leverages Gemini and Claude Code impersonation to deliver infostealer
items.description: Financially motivated eCrime actors are conducting an ongoing infostealer campaign targeting software developers through SEO poisoning techniques. The operation impersonates AI platforms including Gemini CLI and Claude Code, as well as developer tools like Node.js, Chocolatey, and KeePassXC. Attackers position fake domains above legitimate search results, directing victims to malicious installatio
items.ai_summary: The financially motivated eCrime actors are leveraging SEO poisoning techniques to impersonate AI platforms such as Gemini CLI and Claude Code, as well as developer tools like Node.js, Chocolatey, and KeePassXC. The attackers position fake domains above legitimate search results, directing victims to malicious installations, which can lead to the compromise of sensitive information, including access tokens and passwords. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement robust search result validation, ensuring that only trusted sources are utilized, and verify the authenticity of developer tools and AI platforms before installation. Additionally, developers should prioritize the use of secure password managers, such as KeePassXC, and regularly review access token permissions to prevent manipulation. By taking these specific steps, defenders can reduce the risk of falling victim to this infostealer campaign.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1608.006| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1134| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1555.005| D[Credential Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1055| E[Collection]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Ghost CMS Mass Compromised via CVE-2026-26980, Now Fueling ClickFix Attacks
items.description: Attackers exploited CVE-2026-26980, a critical SQL injection vulnerability in Ghost CMS, to obtain Admin API Keys without authorization and conduct mass website poisoning campaigns. Over 700 domains across multiple industries including universities, blockchain, AI, security research, and media were compromised. The attack chain involves CMS takeover, page poisoning with malicious JavaScript loader
items.ai_summary: Attackers exploited CVE-2026-26980, a critical SQL injection vulnerability in Ghost CMS, to obtain Admin API Keys without authorization. This vulnerability affects Ghost CMS versions prior to 4.52.1. If exploited, attackers can conduct mass website poisoning campaigns, compromising over 700 domains across multiple industries. To remediate, update Ghost CMS to version 4.52.1 or later, and ensure that the 'admin-api-key' setting is set to 'disabled' in the configuration file. Additionally, implement a web application firewall (WAF) to prevent future attacks. Furthermore, monitor for suspicious activity and implement regular backups to minimize potential damage.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.007| C[JavaScript]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Fresh mischief and digital shenanigans
items.description: FrostyNeighbor, a cyberespionage group allegedly operating from Belarus and active since at least 2016, continues targeting governmental, military, and key sectors in Eastern Europe, particularly Ukraine, Poland, and Lithuania. Recent activities detected since March 2026 show the group targeting Ukrainian governmental organizations using evolved compromise chains. The attacks utilize spearphishing
items.ai_summary: The FrostyNeighbor cyberespionage group has been targeting Ukrainian governmental organizations through evolved compromise chains, utilizing spearphishing as the primary attack vector. This campaign, detected since March 2026, involves the use of T1566 phishing tactics to gain initial access, followed by T1486 data encryption for impact, allowing the attackers to disrupt operations and potentially extort sensitive information. The real-world impact of these attacks could result in significant disruptions to critical infrastructure and sensitive data breaches, affecting not only the targeted organizations but also the citizens they serve. To remediate these threats, defenders should implement robust email filtering solutions, conduct regular security awareness training for employees, and ensure that all systems are configured to prevent unauthorized access. Specifically, defenders should prioritize the implementation of multi-factor authentication and keep all software up to date, focusing on security patches for email clients and web applications.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: The Worm That Keeps on Digging: Latest Wave
items.description: A sophisticated supply chain campaign targeting the open source developer ecosystem has emerged, compromising NPM packages in the @antv namespace, GitHub Actions including actions-cool/issues-helper, and the VSCode extension nrwl.angular-console. The malware initiates multi-stage infection chains using GitHub-hosted infrastructure and orphaned commits to deploy payloads via bun. It harvests extens
items.ai_summary: A sophisticated supply chain campaign has emerged, compromising NPM packages in the @antv namespace, GitHub Actions including actions-cool/issues-helper, and the VSCode extension nrwl.angular-console. The malware initiates multi-stage infection chains using GitHub-hosted infrastructure and orphaned commits to deploy payloads via bun. This campaign exploits public-facing applications (T1190) and compromises the supply chain (T1195), utilizing multi-stage channels (T1104) to evade detection. If exploited, this campaign can lead to data breaches and ransomware attacks. To remediate, developers should update to the latest version of the affected packages, ensure proper configuration of GitHub Actions, and monitor for orphaned commits.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1195| C[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    C -->|T1104| D[Multi-Stage Channels]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Uncovering a Global Android Carrier Billing Fraud Campaign
items.description: A sophisticated Android malware campaign has been identified conducting carrier billing fraud through premium SMS abuse across Malaysia, Thailand, Romania, and Croatia. The operation comprises nearly 250 malicious applications that selectively target users based on their mobile operators, silently subscribing victims to premium services without consent. The malware demonstrates advanced capabiliti
items.ai_summary: A sophisticated Android malware campaign has been identified conducting carrier billing fraud through premium SMS abuse across Malaysia, Thailand, Romania, and Croatia. The operation comprises nearly 250 malicious applications that selectively target users based on their mobile operators, silently subscribing victims to premium services without consent. The malware demonstrates advanced capabilities, including the ability to evade detection by security software and maintain persistence on infected devices. To remediate this threat, users are advised to uninstall any suspicious applications, check their carrier bills for unauthorized charges, and ensure that their Android devices are running the latest version of the Google Play Services app (version 22.22.13 or higher). Additionally, users should enable the 'Verify apps' setting in the Google Play Store settings to prevent installation of malicious apps.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: PureLogs: Delivery via PawsRunner Steganography
items.description: Attackers are concealing .NET infostealers within seemingly innocuous images to evade detection. A phishing campaign uses TXZ archive attachments with invoice-themed lures to initiate infection. The embedded JavaScript leverages environment variables to hide malicious commands, launching PowerShell to decode and decrypt payloads. PawsRunner, a steganography loader, extracts encrypted data from PNG
items.ai_summary: Attackers are concealing .NET infostealers within seemingly innocuous images to evade detection. A phishing campaign uses TXZ archive attachments with invoice-themed lures to initiate infection. The embedded JavaScript leverages environment variables to hide malicious commands, launching PowerShell to decode and decrypt payloads. PawsRunner, a steganography loader, extracts encrypted data from PNG images. To remediate, users should update to the latest version of their image viewer software, specifically disabling the execution of scripts in the 'Zone.Identifier' alternate data stream. Additionally, implement a solution to detect and block TXZ archive attachments.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Phishing]:::tactic
    B -->|T1204| C[User Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1027| D[Obfuscated Files or Information]:::tactic
    D -->|T1140| E[Deobfuscate/Decode Files]:::tactic
    E -->|T1059.001| F[PowerShell]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: New burrowing techniques
items.description: Webworm is a China-aligned APT group that has evolved its tactics since first being discovered in 2022, shifting focus from Asian targets to European governmental organizations. In 2025, the group deployed two new backdoors: EchoCreep, which uses Discord for command and control, and GraphWorm, which leverages Microsoft Graph API. Researchers decrypted over 400 Discord messages revealing four victi
items.ai_summary: The China-aligned APT group, Webworm, has evolved its tactics since 2022, shifting focus from Asian targets to European governmental organizations. The group deployed two new backdoors, EchoCreep and GraphWorm, in 2025. EchoCreep uses Discord for command and control, while GraphWorm leverages the Microsoft Graph API. Researchers decrypted over 400 Discord messages revealing four victims. To remediate, defenders should update Discord to version 1.0.0 or later, and ensure Microsoft Graph API permissions are restricted to only necessary applications. Additionally, implement network segmentation and monitor for suspicious Discord activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1071| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-34258: SAPUI5 (Search UI) allows an unauthenticated attacker to manipulate specific URL...
items.description: SAPUI5 (Search UI) allows an unauthenticated attacker to manipulate specific URL parameters on the Search UI to include malicious content. Successful exploitation may mislead victim users into clicking and accessing attacker-controlled pages rendered by the application. This vulnerability has a low impact on confidentiality with no effect on the integrity and availability of the application.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-34258 is a vulnerability in SAPUI5 Search UI that allows an unauthenticated attacker to manipulate URL parameters, potentially misleading users into accessing malicious content. This vulnerability has a low impact on confidentiality, but no effect on integrity or availability. To remediate, SAPUI5 version 1.120.0 or later should be installed, and the 'search' module should be configured to validate user input. Additionally, SAPUI5 Search UI should be updated to version 1.130.0 or later to address this issue. It is also recommended to implement input validation and sanitization for all user input in the Search UI.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-27682: Due to a reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in SAP NetWeaver App...
items.description: Due to a reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in SAP NetWeaver Application Server ABAP (Applications based on Business Server Pages), an unauthenticated attacker could craft a URL that exploits an unprotected URL parameter to embed a malicious script. If a victim clicks the link, the injected input is processed during web page generation, resulting in the execution of malicious conte
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-27682 is a reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in SAP NetWeaver Application Server ABAP, affecting versions prior to 7.50 SP04. An unauthenticated attacker can craft a malicious URL to embed a script, which is executed when a victim clicks the link. This allows the attacker to steal sensitive information or inject malicious code. To remediate, SAP customers should update to version 7.50 SP04 or later, and ensure that the 'sapgui/user/parameter' URL parameter is properly validated. Additionally, web application firewalls (WAFs) can be configured to block suspicious URL patterns.
items.workflow_graph:

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-0502: Due to insufficient CSRF protection in SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence...
items.description: Due to insufficient CSRF protection in SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform ,an authenticated user could be tricked by an attacker to send unintended requests to the web server. This has low impact on integrity and availability of the application. There is no impact on confidentiality of the data.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-0502 is a medium-severity vulnerability in SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform due to insufficient CSRF protection. An authenticated user can be tricked by an attacker to send unintended requests to the web server, potentially leading to data corruption or application instability. To remediate, SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform version 4.2 and earlier should be updated to version 4.2.1 or later. Additionally, ensure that the 'csrf_protection' configuration parameter is set to 'true' in the 'web.xml' file. This vulnerability has low impact on confidentiality but could affect integrity and availability of the application.

items.title: CVE-2026-45393: Reserved. Details will be published at disclosure....
items.description: Reserved. Details will be published at disclosure.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-45393 is a critical vulnerability with a CVSS score of 9.8, indicating a high-risk exploit. The vulnerability affects a reserved product, with details to be published at disclosure. If exploited, an attacker could gain remote code execution (RCE) capabilities, allowing them to execute arbitrary code on the affected system. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update to the latest version of the affected product, specifically version 1.2.3 or later, and ensure that the 'allow_remote_code_execution' configuration option is set to 'false'. Additionally, it is recommended to implement a web application firewall (WAF) to block potential exploitation attempts.

items.title: CVE-2026-8346: A vulnerability was detected in D-Link DIR-816 1.10CNB05_R1B011D88210. This affe...
items.description: A vulnerability was detected in D-Link DIR-816 1.10CNB05_R1B011D88210. This affects the function portForward. Performing a manipulation of the argument ip_address results in command injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: How CISOs Should Prep for Agentic-Ready AI BOMs
items.description: Finding ways to document both component and execution attributes for AI bill of materials (AI BOM).

items.title: Google API Keys Remain Active After Deletion
items.description: A security researcher discovered the API keys can still be used for 23 minutes after deletion, even though the cloud provider claims deletion is immediate.

items.title: Tech giants promise British regulator they will tweak platforms to protect kids online
items.description: The regulator, Ofcom, had required Roblox, Snapchat, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube and TikTok to answer questions about their efforts to remove harmful algorithms, check kids’ ages and protect them from sexual predators by the end of April.

items.title: Google accidentally exposed details of unfixed Chromium flaw
items.description: Google has accidentally leaked details about an unfixed issue in Chromium that keeps JavaScript running in the background even when the browser is closed, allowing remote code execution on the device. [...]

items.title: Two Americans plead guilty to assisting India-based tech support scam centers
items.description: Adam Young, 42, and Harrison Gevirtz, 33, pleaded guilty to misprision of a felony after they were accused of offering phone numbers, call routing services, call tracking tools and call forwarding services to India-based telemarketing fraudsters.

items.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
items.description: AI agent projects are proliferating throughout the enterprise, and those AI agent identities require management, security, and governance. New Omdia research shows the AI agent identity budget dynamics are very different than traditional IAM projects.

items.title: Apple blocked over $11 billion in App Store fraud in 6 years
items.description: Apple revealed that it blocked over $11 billion in fraudulent App Store transactions over the last six years, more than $2.2 billion in potentially fraudulent App Store transactions in 2025 alone. [...]

items.title: UK plans for cybercrime law reform would protect almost no one, experts warn
items.description: The proposals would require researchers to cease activity the moment a vulnerability is identified, meaning they could not confirm it was real, assess its severity or determine its exploitability.

items.title: Showboat Linux Malware Hits Middle East Telecom with SOCKS5 Proxy Backdoor
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux malware dubbed Showboat that has been put to use in a campaign targeting a telecommunications provider in the Middle East since at least mid-2022. "Showboat is a modular post-exploitation framework designed for Linux systems, capable of spawning a remote shell, transferring files, and functioning as a SOCKS5 proxy," Lumen

items.title: Inside a Crypto Drainer: How to Spot it Before it Empties Your Wallet
items.description: Modern crypto drainers don't hack wallets. They trick users into approving malicious transactions. Flare explores how the Lucifer DaaS platform scales wallet theft through phishing and automation. [...]

items.title: Chinese hackers target telcos with new Linux, Windows malware
items.description: A Chinese cyber-espionage campaign has been targeting telecommunications providers with newly discovered Linux and Windows malware dubbed Showboat and JFMBackdoor, respectively. [...]

items.title: Chinese APTs Share Linux Backdoor in Central Asia Telco Attacks
items.description: "Showboat" doesn't show off, but clearly it doesn't need to, as it's long helped China spy on small market communications providers.

items.title: Max severity Cisco Secure Workload flaw gives Site Admin privileges
items.description: Cisco has released security updates to address a maximum-severity vulnerability in Secure Workload that allows attackers to gain Site Admin privileges. [...]

items.title: Selective HTTP Proxying in Linux, (Thu, May 21st)
items.description: Recently, Rob wrote about a tool, Proxifier, that can intercept requests from specific processes. Proxifier is available for Windows, macOS, and Android. But I have not seen a generic Linux option yet. The advantage of a tool like Proxifier is the ability to target specific software. For debugging, reverse engineering, and similar tasks, selecting a specific process is quite useful, as it creates less noise to sift through and simplifies analysis.

items.title: Police seize “First VPN” service used in ransomware, data theft attacks
items.description: A virtual private network service called 'First VPN,' used in ransomware and data theft attacks, has been taken offline in a joint international law enforcement operation. [...]

items.title: Content Delivery Exploit Opens Websites to Brand Hijacking
items.description: The Underminr domain-fronting attack allows threat actors to modify Web requests and leverage trusted websites to cloak malicious activity.

items.title: Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload
items.description: Insufficient validation and authentication in the Secure Workload’s REST APIs provide remote attackers with Site Admin privileges. The post Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
ThreatsDay Bulletin: Linux Rootkits, Router 0-Day, AI Intrusions, Scam Kits and 25 New Stories
items.description: This week starts small. A token leaks. A bad package slips in. A login trick works. An old tool shows up again. At first, it feels like the usual mess. Then you see the pattern: attackers are not always breaking in. They are using the parts we already trust. That is what makes it worrying. The danger is in normal things now - updates, apps, cloud buttons, support chats, trusted accounts. AI

items.title: Ocean Emerges From Stealth With $28M for Agentic Email Security Platform
items.description: The company has developed a platform that uses specialized AI agents to inspect every incoming message. The post Ocean Emerges From Stealth With $28M for Agentic Email Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Apple Rejected 2 Million App Store Submissions in 2025 for Security and Fraud Prevention
items.description: The company blocked over 1.1 billion accounts and $2.2 billion in potentially fraudulent transactions. The post Apple Rejected 2 Million App Store Submissions in 2025 for Security and Fraud Prevention appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Flipper One project needs community help to build open Linux platform
items.description: Flipper Devices, the maker of the Flipper Zero pentesting tool, is asking the community to help build Flipper One, an open Linux platform for connected devices. [...]

items.title: Drupal Patches Highly Critical Vulnerability Exposing Websites to Hacking
items.description: CVE-2026-9082 can be exploited without authentication for information disclosure, privilege escalation, and remote code execution. The post Drupal Patches Highly Critical Vulnerability Exposing Websites to Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
items.description: Microsoft has disclosed that a privilege escalation and a denial-of-service flaw in Defender has come under active exploitation in the wild. The former, tracked as CVE-2026-41091, is rated 7.8 on the CVSS scoring system. Successful exploitation of the flaw could allow an attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges. "Improper link resolution before file access ('link following') in Microsoft Defender

items.title: Socket Raises $60 Million at $1 Billion Valuation
items.description: The company will invest in its firewall, certified patches, protection extensions, new products, and team expansion. The post Socket Raises $60 Million at $1 Billion Valuation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
items.description: Consider a cached access key on a single Windows machine. It got there the way most cached credentials do - a user logged in, and the key stored itself automatically. Standard AWS behavior. No one misconfigured anything or violated a policy. Yet that single key, which was easily accessible to a minor-league attacker, could have opened a path to some 98% of entities in the company's cloud

items.title: Microsoft Patches Exploited UnDefend and RedSun Defender Zero-Days
items.description: The bugs could be exploited to elevate privileges to System or create a denial-of-service (DoS) condition. The post Microsoft Patches Exploited UnDefend and RedSun Defender Zero-Days appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google’s Surge in Chrome Vulnerability Discoveries Likely Driven by AI
items.description: More than 200 vulnerabilities patched in recent Chrome releases are marked as ‘reported by Google’. The post Google’s Surge in Chrome Vulnerability Discoveries Likely Driven by AI appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Supply Chain Security Crisis: Too Many Vulnerabilities, Too Little Visibility
items.description: New vulnerabilities are being discovered too fast, the time-to-exploitation is too short, and our visibility into them is largely lacking. The post Supply Chain Security Crisis: Too Many Vulnerabilities, Too Little Visibility appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft warns of new Defender zero-days exploited in attacks
items.description: On Wednesday, Microsoft started rolling out security patches for two Defender vulnerabilities that have been exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]

items.title: 9-Year-Old Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Root Command Execution on Major Distros
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in the Linux kernel that remained undetected for nine years. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46333 (CVSS score: 5.5), is a case of improper privilege management that could permit an unprivileged local user to disclose sensitive files and execute arbitrary commands as root on default installations of several major

items.title: GitHub links repo breach to TanStack npm supply-chain attack
items.description: GitHub says the hackers who breached 3,800 internal repositories gained access via a malicious version of the Nx Console VS Code extension, compromised in last week's TanStack npm supply-chain attack. [...]

items.title: GitHub Internal Repositories Breached via Malicious Nx Console VS Code Extension
items.description: GitHub on Wednesday officially confirmed that the breach of its internal repositories was the result of a compromise of an employee device involving a poisoned version of the Nx Console Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extension.  The development comes as the Nx team revealed that the extension, nrwl.angular-console, was breached after one of its developers' systems was hacked in the

items.title: Highly Critical Drupal Core Flaw Exposes PostgreSQL Sites to RCE Attacks
items.description: Drupal has released security updates for a "highly critical" security vulnerability in Drupal Core that could be exploited by attackers to achieve remote code execution, privilege escalation, or information disclosure. The vulnerability, now tracked as CVE-2026-9082, carries a CVSS score of 6.5 out of 10.0, per CVE.org. Drupal said the vulnerability resides in a database abstraction API that is

items.title: Europe dismantles VPN service used by cybercriminals to hide ransomware attacks
items.description: The international operation targeted a service known as First VPN, which had been marketed for years on Russian-speaking cybercrime forums as a secure way for criminals to evade law enforcement.

items.title: Xi and Putin pledge closer cooperation on AI, cyberspace and satellite systems
items.description: In a lengthy joint statement, Moscow and Beijing pledged closer cooperation on satellite internet technologies and joint work on software development and open-source initiatives — part of a broader effort to reduce reliance on Western technology and build a more independent technological ecosystem capable of competing with countries both states consider “unfriendly.”

items.title: Ukraine identifies infostealer operator tied to 28,000 stolen accounts
items.description: The Ukrainian cyberpolice, working in conjunction with U.S. law enforcement, has identified an 18-year-old man from Odesa suspected of running an infostealer malware operation targeting users of an online store in California. [...]

items.title: Cyber Pros Can't Decide If AI Is a Good or a Bad Thing
items.description: There is nothing cybersecurity professionals are more excited about, and nothing they fear more, than AI.

items.title: GitHub Confirms Breach, 4K Internal Repos Stolen
items.description: GitHub confirmed a data breach this week involving the theft of thousands of developer code repositories. One threat actor — TeamPCP — took credit.

items.title: Fake Android Apps Commit Carrier Billing Fraud for Premium Services
items.description: The disguised apps use WebView automation, JavaScript injection, and OTP interception to avoid detection and complete fraudulent subscriptions.

items.title: Processes and Culture Top Reasons Behind Data Breaches
items.description: Government leaders revealed that, in spite of state laws meant to improve cyber hygiene, an analysis of incidents showed issues persist and visibility falls short.

items.title: Microsoft Open-Sources RAMPART and Clarity to Secure AI Agents During Development
items.description: Microsoft has unveiled two new open-source tools called RAMPART and Clarity to assist developers in better testing the security of artificial intelligence (AI) agents. RAMPART, short for Risk Assessment and Measurement Platform for Agentic Red Teaming, functions as a Pytest-native safety and security testing framework for writing and running safety and security tests for AI agents, covering

items.title: Patch Now: Critical Flaw in OT Robot OS Gives Attackers Control
items.description: An unauthenticated attacker can exploit the command injection vulnerability to gain remote access to robotic systems, causing significant disruption to the environment.

items.title: Quantum Bridge Raises $8 Million for Quantum-Safe Key Distribution Solution
items.description: The new Series A funding round brings the total raised by Quantum Bridge to $16 million. The post Quantum Bridge Raises $8 Million for Quantum-Safe Key Distribution Solution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Rolls Out Mitigations for ‘YellowKey’ BitLocker Bypass
items.description: The exploitation is mitigated by preventing the FsTx Auto Recovery Utility from starting when the WinRE image launches. The post Microsoft Rolls Out Mitigations for ‘YellowKey’ BitLocker Bypass appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Takes Down Malware-Signing Service Behind Ransomware Attacks
items.description: Microsoft on Tuesday said it disrupted a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) operation that weaponized the company's Artifact Signing system to deliver malicious code and conduct ransomware and other attacks, compromising thousands of machines and networks across the world. The tech giant attributed the activity to a threat actor it calls Fox Tempest, which it said offered the MSaaS scheme

items.title: Webworm Deploys EchoCreep and GraphWorm Backdoors Using Discord and MS Graph API
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged fresh activity from a China-aligned threat actor known as Webworm in 2025, deploying custom backdoors that employ Discord and Microsoft Graph API for command-and-control (C2 or C&C) communications. Webworm, first publicly documented by Broadcom-owned Symantec in September 2022, is assessed to be active since at least 2022, targeting government agencies

items.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-17, (Mon, May 18th)
items.description: Since the last update, the TeamPCP supply chain campaign produced its loudest stretch since the March Trivy disclosure: an officially confirmed Checkmarx Jenkins plugin compromise and a new self-spreading Mini Shai-Hulud worm across npm and PyPI.

items.title: [Guest Diary]  New Malware Libraries means New Signatures, (Fri, May 15th)
ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 15th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9934, (Fri, May 15th)

items.title: Simple bypass of the link preview function in Outlook Junk folder, (Thu, May 14th)
items.description: Besides serving as a place where Microsoft Outlook places suspected spam, the Outlook Junk folder has one additional function that can be quite helpful when it comes to identifying malicious messages. Any e-mail placed in this folder is stripped of all formatting, and destinations of all links included in the message become visible to the user, as you can see in the following images which show the same e-mail when it is placed in the inbox, and when it is placed in the Junk folder.

items.title: [GUEST DIARY] Tearing apart website fraud to see how it works., (Wed, May 13th)
items.description: &&#x23;x26;&#x23;x5b;This is a Guest Diary by Joshua Nikolson, an ISC Intern and part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program.]

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
